title: Chapter 7: Work, Energy and Power - Study Notes
source: A Quick Review of E-PCM for Engineering Entrance - Physics

sections.heading: Work (W)
sections.content: Work is the dot product of force and displacement. W = F dot d. It is a scalar quantity. Unit: joule (J). Dimensional formula: [ML²T⁻²]. Signs: (i) If angle θ < 90°, w = +ve. (ii) If θ = 90°, w = 0. (iii) If θ > 90°, w = -ve.
formulas: W = F⃗ · d⃗ = Fdcosθ (constant force)
W = ∫F_x dx (one-dimensional variable force)
W = ∫F_x dx + ∫F_y dy (two-dimensional variable force)
W = ∫F_x dx + ∫F_y dy + ∫F_z dz (three-dimensional variable force)
W = -½Kx² (spring force: compression or elongation from natural position)
W = +½Kx² (spring force: when released from compressed/elongated position)
specialCases: Conservative force: total work done in round trip = 0; work is path-independent (point function). Examples: spring force, gravity, central forces.
Non-conservative force: total work done in round trip ≠ 0; work is path-dependent (path function).

sections.heading: Kinetic Energy and Momentum Relations
sections.content: Kinetic energy K = ½mv² = P²/2m, where P = mv is linear momentum. K is always positive. K ∝ P², so K₁/K₂ = (P₁/P₂)². For small percentage changes (<10%): %ΔKE = 2 × %ΔP.
formulas: K = ½mv² = P²/2m
K₁/K₂ = (P₁/P₂)²
%KE = 2 × %P (for small changes < 10%)
%K = (n² - 1) × 100, where n = 1 + x/100 (if P increased by x%)
%K = (1 - n²) × 100, where n = 1 - x/100 (if P decreased by x%)
specialCases: If two bodies are released with equal KE: P ∝ √m, so P₁/P₂ = √(m₁/m₂).
If two bodies have equal linear momentum: K ∝ 1/m, so K₁/K₂ = m₂/m₁.
If bullet is fired from gun (momentum conserved): K_gun/K_bullet = m_bullet/m_gun < 1, so K_gun < K_bullet.
Example: If momentum increased by 50%, n = 1.5, %ΔKE = (1.5² - 1) × 100 = 125%.

sections.heading: Potential Energy (U)
sections.content: Energy due to position or configuration of a body. Gravitational potential energy of a body of mass m at height h above earth's surface: ΔU = mgh(R/(R+h)).
formulas: ΔU = mgh(R/(R + h)) (general formula)
ΔU = mgR × n/(1 + n) (if h = nR)
ΔU = mgh (if h << R)
ΔU = mg(ℓ/2)sinθ (uniform rod of length ℓ rotated by angle θ with horizontal)
U = ½Kx² (potential energy stored in spring)
U = F²/2K (spring stretched by same force F)
U₁/U₂ = K₂/K₁ (same force applied to two springs)
U₁/U₂ = K₁/K₂ (same elongation in two springs)
specialCases: Energy units: 1 J = 10⁷ erg; 1 calorie = 4.2 J; 1 eV = 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁹ J.
KE is always positive; PE can be positive, negative, or zero.

sections.heading: Work-Energy Theorem
sections.content: Work done by all forces (external and internal) changes the kinetic energy of the body. ΣW = ΔK = K_f - K_i. Work done by external force changes total energy. For conservative force system: ΔK + ΔU = 0, i.e., ΔK = -ΔU. Loss in KE = Gain in PE and vice versa.
formulas: ΣW = ΔK = ½mv_f² - ½mv_i²
ΔK + ΔU = 0 (conservative system)
ΔK = -ΔU
specialCases: If KE of body increases, w = +ve.
If KE of body decreases, w = -ve.
If KE of body is constant, w = 0.
Work done by external force only changes the total energy of the body.

sections.heading: Power (P)
sections.content: Power is the rate of doing work. P = work done / time. It is a scalar. Unit: watt (W) = J/s. Instantaneous power: P = F⃗ · v⃗ = Fvcosθ.
formulas: P = W/t = F⃗ · d⃗/t
P_instantaneous = dW/dt = F⃗ · v⃗ = Fvcosθ
P_average = ΔW/Δt = ∫P dt / ∫dt
specialCases: If a machine delivers constant power: v ∝ t^(1/2), s ∝ t^(3/2), a ∝ t^(-1/2), v ∝ s^(1/3).
If constant force acts on body: P ∝ t (since v = at, so P = Fv = Fat ∝ t).
Stopping distance under brakes: S = V²/2a, i.e., S ∝ V² (for same deceleration).

sections.heading: Special Applications
sections.content: Important derived results for common mechanics problems involving bullets, chains, and pendulums.
formulas: N = n²/(2n-1) (number of planks needed to stop bullet that loses 1/n of velocity in one plank)
T = (n²/(2n-1)) × t (total thickness of penetration)
W = mgℓ/(2n²) = mgℓ₀²/(2ℓ) (work to put hanging chain back on table; 1/n fraction hanging)
V = √(g/ℓ)(ℓ² - ℓ₀²) = √(gℓ(1 - 1/n²)) (velocity when chain completely slips off table)
cosθ = 1 - m²v²/[2gℓ(M+m)] (bullet embeds in block; max angle of string)
Height raised by block h = m²v²/[2g(M+m)²]
Tension in string T = (M + m)g cosθ
cosθ = 1 - m²(v - v_b)²/[2gℓ(M+m)] (bullet passes through block with velocity v_b)
specialCases: Example: Bullet loses 1/20 of velocity in plank of 2 cm → N = 20²/(2×20-1) = 400/39 ≈ 10.256, so N = 11 planks; total penetration T = 10.256 × 2 = 20.51 cm.
For chain on table with 1/n hanging: work to pull up = mgℓ/(2n²).
Maximum KE of pendulum bob at mean position = mgl(1 - cosθ) where θ is max angular displacement.
